2606.12834.title: Fantastic Scientific Agents and How to Build Them: AgentBuild for Rietveld Refinement
2606.12834.paper_summary: As scientific workflows shift from deterministic executables to LLM-based agents, the development practices on offer, such as fine-tuning, reinforcement learning, and prompt-and-go, bury the scientist's judgment. We propose treating agent construction as a workflow stage and introduce AgentBuild, which builds a scientific agent from a contract the scientist authors. The contract is a version-controlled rubric, a difficulty-graded curriculum, and a curated external knowledge base. A rubric-driven judge gates a meta-optimizer coding agent that edits the agent within a declared boundary, so the build compiles the agent, not the scientist's judgment. We instantiate this for Rietveld refinement of X-ray diffraction data through GSAS-II behind MCP and A2A, where a blank-harness construction run progresses through a lithium lanthanum zirconium oxide (LLZO) signal-to-noise ladder, reaches the 4 hour scan as a frontier case, and exposes the workflow-scope limits that remain. The same rubric that rewards credible fits also scores trajectory scope, making the frontier a contract failure rather than a pattern-fitting failure. As base models evolve, re-running AgentBuild is a re-tune, not a rebuild, and the scientist's authored contract remains the durable asset.
2606.12834.paper_summary_zh: 隨著科學工作流程從確定性可執行檔轉向基於大型語言模型的代理，所提供的開發實踐，例如微調、強化學習和即時提示，掩埋了科學家的判斷。<lb/>我們建議將代理構建視為工作流程的一個階段，並引入AgentBuild，該工具從科學家撰寫的合約中構建科學代理。<lb/>該合約是一個版本控制的評分標準、一個難度分級的課程，以及一個策劃的外部知識庫。<lb/>基於評分標準的評判者控制著一個元優化器編碼代理，該代理在聲明的邊界內編輯代理，因此構建的是代理，而不是科學家的判斷。<lb/>我們為X射線衍射數據的Rietveld精修實現了這一點，通過MCP和A2A的GSAS-II，當一個空白的裝置構建運行通過鋰鋯鋁氧化物（LLZO）信號與噪聲的階梯，達到4小時掃描作為前沿案例，並揭示了仍然存在的工作流程範圍限制。<lb/>同樣的評分標準不僅獎勵可信的擬合，還評分軌跡範圍，使前沿成為合約失敗而非模式擬合失敗。<lb/>隨著基礎模型的演變，重新運行AgentBuild是一種重新調整，而不是重建，科學家撰寫的合約仍然是持久的資產。
2606.12834.author: Woong Shin et.al.
2606.12834.authors: Woong Shin, Craig A. Bridges, Marshall T. McDonnell, Rafael Ferreira da Silva
